WU1.description: NEW helper scripts/epistemic-marker.sh: epistemic_claude_main_pid (process-tree traversal with caching), epistemic_marker_path, epistemic_get_session_id, epistemic_session_active, epistemic_sweep_orphans (two-condition check), epistemic_write_marker

WU2.description: Refactor epistemic-preflight.sh: stdin session_id, source helper, sweep+migrate+source-branching (startup/resume/clear/default→stderr-warn-as-startup). NO uuidgen fallback.

WU3.description: Refactor epistemic-postflight.sh: helper-based read, stdin session_id fallback (verified by probe v2), scoped delete on own marker only

WU5a.description: commands/epistemic-preflight.md: helper-based read; rewrite create-fallback to per-claude-PID directory (CF-4 M1)
WU5a.tdd_skip_reason: Mechanical refactor with one design fix (fallback rewrite)

WU5b.description: commands/epistemic-postflight.md: helper-based read; rewrite Step 6 cleanup to scoped delete (CF-4 M4)

WU5c.description: commands/end.md: helper-based read; rewrite line-284 grep that doesn't match UUIDs (CF-4 M2)

WU5d.description: commands/{start,collect-insights,vault-curate}.md: replace -f checks with epistemic_session_active; helper-based read (CF-4 M3)

WU6.description: Update prose references in 7 docs (added docs/PLANNING-STORAGE.md per CF-17; fix describe.md 7-vs-9 hooks per CF-19)

WU8.description: Parallel-isolation eval fixture (AC1, AC2) — subshell with PPID propagation simulating two claude processes

WU9.description: Resume-pairing eval fixture (AC3 proxy; manual verification authoritative)

WU10.description: Orphan-sweep eval fixture (AC5 — fake PID + PID-reuse defense)

WU11.description: Migration eval fixture (AC6) including ENOENT race path

WU12.description: Extend test.sh: Category 1 syntax + Category 2 shellcheck + Category 5 no-set-e for new helper; verify install dry run (CF-15)

batches.rationale: No dependencies — helper creation and pure-doc updates parallel
All depend on WU1; refactor surfaces are disjoint files. WU5a-5d split (was WU5 in rev1) enumerates per-file fix types per CF-4.
Eval/test additions depend on WU2 and/or WU3

file_conflicts.note: All four eval-add WUs touch the same JSON file — must be sequenced. Recommend sequential execution within batch 2.

analysis.parallelization_note: execution_preference=simplicity. Sequential is recommended. WU5a-5d are truly independent (different files) and could run in parallel via /delegate, but each is small.

rev1_diff_summary.split: WU5 → WU5a/b/c/d (per-file fix enumeration per CF-4)
